title: Permissions & Access
summary: Assign roles, permission levels, and hierarchy placement — and confirm multi-office access works for users who need it.
customerSummary: We give each person the right role and access, including access across multiple offices where you need it.
overview:
Permissions decide what each user can see and do. Assign the correct **role and permission level** to every user per the intake structure, and place them at the right level of the org hierarchy.

Users of a parent org that has multiple offices can **switch into any child office** from **My Profile → Other Accounts**, or via **Settings → Company**. Confirm that access works for anyone who needs to move between offices.
prerequisites: Intended permission level documented per rep from intake
tips: Apply least privilege: grant the lowest permission level that lets the person do their job.
Parent-org users switch offices from My Profile → Other Accounts, or Settings → Company.
Spot-check permissions by logging in as representative users across different orgs.
warnings: The specific roles and permission levels available (and what each grants) are not fully documented in the source — see Process Gaps B-6. Confirm with CS if unsure.
commonMistakes: Over-granting admin-level access to standard reps.
Forgetting to enable multi-office access for managers who cover several offices.
Assigning a permission level that doesn't match what intake specified.

steps.title: Confirm multi-office access
steps.description: For parent-org users who cover multiple offices, confirm they can switch into child offices via My Profile → Other Accounts and via Settings → Company.
steps.verify: A test user can switch into a child office and back to their root account.

callouts.text: Send users who cover multiple offices the [Access Other Offices guide (PDF)](/guides/access-other-offices.pdf).

screenshots.caption: My Profile — Root Account on top, Other Accounts below; switch via the row arrow.
Settings → Company — office type, users, phone, and address; direct login per row.
